records.ety: [L. inflectere, inflexum; pref. in- in + flectere to bend. See Flexible, and cf. Inflex.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Inflected; p. pr. & vb. n. Inflecting.]

defs: To turn from a direct line or course; to bend; to incline, to deflect; to curve; to bow.
quotes: Are they [the rays of the sun] not reflected, refracted, and inflected by one and the same principle ?

defs: To vary, as a noun or a verb in its terminations; to decline, as a noun or adjective, or to conjugate, as a verb.
